title: Fluent Bit Chunk Flush Latency P95 Trending
app: Fluent Bit HTTP `/api/v1/storage` + `/api/v1/metrics` scraped to Splunk
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentbit:storage" OR sourcetype="fluentbit:metrics"
| eval flush_ms=coalesce(flush_time_ms, flush_duration_ms, if(isnotnull(chunk_flush_time_ns), chunk_flush_time_ns/1000000, null()))
| where isnotnull(flush_ms)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats perc95(flush_ms) as p95_flush, count as n by _time, output_name, host
| eventstats median(p95_flush) as med by output_name
| where p95_flush > med * 2 AND n > 20
| sort -p95_flush
description: Rising flush latency means outputs (Splunk HEC, Kafka, S3) are slowing or network RTT is degrading; p95 catches tail pain that averages hide. Early warning prevents chunk backlog growth on edge agents.
value: Stabilizes edge logging on constrained hardware and prevents storm-induced drops.
implementation: Normalize nanoseconds to milliseconds once at ingest. If metrics lack per-output latency, approximate with `storage_chunks.up` age fields. Correlate with regional network dashboards.
visualization: Line chart (p95 by output), heatmap (host × output), single-value (worst p95).
grandmaExplanation: We read the forwarder and buffer logs for stress and give-ups so we see log loss risk before disks or patience run out.
knownFalsePositives: Buffer pain from downstream saturation, bad TLS, or local disk. We check agent and destination before blind buffer tuning.
